title: app_identity: Redundant memset before struct copy in upsert_peer
description:
File: lichen/subsys/lichen/app_identity/app_identity.c:224-225

The code performs:
  memset(&s_peers[slot].peer, 0, sizeof(s_peers[slot].peer));
  s_peers[slot].peer = *peer;

The memset zeros the entire struct, then the struct assignment immediately overwrites every byte with data from *peer. The memset is completely redundant and wastes CPU cycles.

The subsequent copy_string call at line 226-228 sanitizes display_name by zero-padding after the NUL terminator, but that's a separate operation from the redundant memset.

Suggested fix: Remove the memset at line 224.
